title: The Thief Of Baghead

jokes.text: It seems someone in this room... Is a murderer! It's me.
jokes.setup: Dramatic murder mystery setup with classic 'one of you is the murderer' trope
jokes.punchline: Immediate anticlimatic confession that it's him
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with perfect economy. The immediate confession subverts the mystery genre perfectly.

jokes.text: Calculon really shatnered the hell out of that scene.
jokes.punchline: Using 'Shatner' as a verb for overacting
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay turning an actor's name into a verb for overacting. Quotable and well-crafted.

jokes.text: Aquariums have really changed since I was a boy and no one ever took me to them.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of childhood neglect
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - Fry's oblivious way of revealing sad childhood details. Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: so there's nothing for kids to smear yogurt on. I like strawberry yogurt.
jokes.setup: Explaining the lack of glass in future aquariums
jokes.punchline: Random yogurt preference after mentioning kids smearing yogurt
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor showing Fry's childlike associations and non-sequitur thinking.

jokes.text: Can I touch the water? I wouldn't, but I'm smart.
jokes.setup: Fry asking if he can touch the water
jokes.punchline: Professor's backhanded insult about intelligence
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor Farnsworth condescension, implying Fry lacks intelligence to make good decisions.

jokes.text: Welcome... To Jurassic tank.
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'Jurassic Park' but with aquarium tank
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference pun. Functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Let me just extend the old third leg.
jokes.setup: Needing a tripod for the camera
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo about 'third leg' while literally extending a tripod leg
jokes.explanation: Predictable double entendre that works because it's literally accurate for Bender's robot body.

jokes.text: Ooh, yeah. That's stable, baby.
jokes.punchline: Sexual tone while talking about camera stability
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the innuendo joke but less clever. Relies on Bender's personality.

jokes.text: Twilight zone one-minute-in-the-future lens? Ooh, Calculon's going to be here.
jokes.punchline: A lens that can see one minute into the future, immediately proving itself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant sci-fi comedy concept. The lens immediately demonstrates its own functionality, creating perfect circular logic.

jokes.text: Film? Who uses film? We've had digital cameras for a thousand years.
jokes.punchline: Fry, from the past, questioning outdated technology in the future
jokes.explanation: Great character irony - the person from the past criticizing someone for using old technology. Perfect role reversal.

jokes.text: Your eyes are digital cameras.
jokes.setup: Argument about digital vs film photography
jokes.punchline: Pointing out that robot/cyborg eyes are literally digital cameras
jokes.explanation: Smart observation about the sci-fi setting. Bender making a logical point about digital vs analog in their world.

jokes.text: You're looking at the last chemical darkroom in existence.
jokes.setup: Question about where to develop film in the future
jokes.punchline: Bender's torso happens to contain the only darkroom left
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - his body containing something improbable but useful. Great character-specific absurdism.

jokes.text: Yo, Calky! I'm a big fan, and I've got a big camera.
jokes.punchline: Rhyming 'big fan' with 'big camera' in crude celebrity approach
jokes.explanation: Simple rhyme and casual approach to celebrity. Fits Bender's character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Try one where you're pretending to swing a tennis racquet.
jokes.punchline: Specific, random direction for fake candid photo
jokes.explanation: Mild satire of paparazzi directing fake candid shots. The specificity of tennis racquet adds some humor.

jokes.text: These rare and fascinating deep-sea fish are extremely sensitive to light
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by Bender's flash photography
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for immediate contradiction. The timing makes the structural humor work well.

jokes.text: Come on, smile! Quit blinking! Red eye! Blurry! Lens cap's still on!
jokes.setup: Bender trying to get the perfect photo
jokes.punchline: Series of increasingly basic photography failures
jokes.explanation: Good escalation ending with the most basic mistake. Captures amateur photographer frustration.

jokes.text: At last, we can enjoy our studio-mandated quality time.
jokes.setup: Escaping the paparazzi with his TV family
jokes.punchline: Revealing the family time is contractually obligated
jokes.explanation: Good satire of celebrity fake relationships and contractual obligations. 'Studio-mandated' is perfectly cynical.

jokes.text: Man, I've never had anyone try so hard to digest me.
jokes.setup: Being inside a sea monster's stomach
jokes.punchline: Casual observation about the digestion attempt's enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Bender's casual attitude toward life-threatening situations. The phrasing 'try so hard' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: These pictures are just as good as the ones in my celebrity tabloid. Us people magazine?
jokes.punchline: 'Us People' magazine - parody of 'Us' and 'People' magazines combined
jokes.explanation: Clever combination of two real celebrity magazine names. Simple but effective wordplay.

jokes.text: Well, I changed my mind and came to Hollywood after all.
jokes.setup: Previously claiming no time for foolishness
jokes.punchline: Immediately showing up in Hollywood anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - completely contradicting himself for self-interest with zero self-awareness.

jokes.text: Namely, a camera.
jokes.setup: Explaining Bender might have what it takes to be a paparazzo
jokes.punchline: The only qualification being literally just owning a camera
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery reducing the glamorous paparazzi job to its most basic requirement.

jokes.text: Hmm, I don't know. How much will it cost me?
jokes.punchline: Thinking he has to pay to work
jokes.explanation: Simple misunderstanding humor. Shows Bender's occasional confusion about how employment works.

jokes.text: Sir, you just hired yourself a new whatsa-whatzo.
jokes.punchline: Still not knowing the correct term after it was explained
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Bender never learned the word. Character consistency in his selective listening.

jokes.text: I want you all to know that I'm already better than you. Stay out of my way!
jokes.setup: Being introduced to fellow paparazzi on first day
jokes.punchline: Immediately declaring superiority and hostility
jokes.explanation: Pure Bender ego and arrogance. Perfect character integration - this is exactly what he'd do on day one.

jokes.text: No, not that scene-chewing ham. I said the greatest actor... Langdon Cobb.
jokes.setup: Bender thinking Calculon is the greatest actor
jokes.punchline: Leela dismissing Calculon as a ham, preferring the mysterious Langdon Cobb
jokes.explanation: 'Scene-chewing ham' is a perfect theatrical insult. Sets up the contrast between the two actors.

jokes.text: Let's rewind, shall we? Let's rewind again.
jokes.punchline: Keeps rewinding to hear the same praise
jokes.explanation: Shows Zoidberg's obsession and need for validation, decent repetition gag

jokes.text: He's raked in seven academy awards for best actor and one for best actress
jokes.punchline: Winning both male and female acting awards
jokes.explanation: Absurd achievement that raises questions about the character's identity/gender. Good setup for mystery.

jokes.text: For Rigel 7... And all mankind!
jokes.punchline: Patriotic speech for alien planet Rigel 7 instead of Earth
jokes.explanation: Simple sci-fi joke substituting alien planet for Earth in heroic speech. Fits the Futurama universe.

jokes.text: No, Daisy, don't! I tried to warn you. I'm hideous. To me, you're beautiful.
jokes.punchline: The dialogue mirrors the real situation - hidden appearance and beauty
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor where the fictional scene parallels the actor's real situation. Good layered writing.

jokes.text: One 60-foot ladder, please.
jokes.punchline: Bender's solution is a slightly taller ladder
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - solving high-tech security with simple brute force approach. Great economy.

jokes.text: What are you, some kind of attack fungus? Or just a moldy schnauzer?
jokes.punchline: Two equally absurd possibilities for what it might be
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between threatening 'attack fungus' and mundane 'moldy schnauzer.' Both equally dismissive.

jokes.text: Oh, God, please let this tacky Hollywood mansion have the obligatory... Yes! Tuscan-inspired pergola!
jokes.punchline: Hoping for and finding the most stereotypical Hollywood mansion feature
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of Hollywood mansion clichés. 'Tuscan-inspired pergola' is perfectly specific and pretentious.

jokes.text: Peace out, Portobello.
jokes.punchline: Casual goodbye using mushroom variety name
jokes.explanation: Clever mushroom pun with perfect casual delivery. Very quotable and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I swear on my honor as a paparazzo.
jokes.setup: Promising to never show the photo
jokes.punchline: Swearing on the honor of a profession known for having no honor
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - swearing on the honor of a dishonorable profession. Great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: Hey, Fry, want to see a picture? Sure.
jokes.setup: Bender just promised never to show the photo
jokes.punchline: Immediately breaking his promise in the next scene
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback showing Bender's complete lack of integrity. The timing makes it perfect - literally the next scene.

jokes.text: Visual gag: Character looks at photo and immediately deflates like a balloon
jokes.setup: Fry agreeing to look at photo
jokes.punchline: Immediately and literally deflates upon seeing it
jokes.explanation: Strong visual comedy with immediate cause-and-effect. Establishes the photo's supernatural power.

jokes.text: But... it could just be a coincidence.
jokes.setup: Fry deflates after looking at the photo
jokes.punchline: Bender considering it might be coincidence despite obvious cause and effect
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's denial despite clear evidence. Underplays the obvious.

jokes.text: I'd feel more comfortable raising the alarm if I were a little more certain.
jokes.setup: Two people deflated from the photo
jokes.punchline: Still needing more evidence despite clear pattern
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Bender's denial. Mock-scientific approach to obvious supernatural phenomenon.

jokes.text: Now, this I want to see as clearly as possible.
jokes.setup: Warning about dangerous photo of Langdon Cobb
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg wanting to see it extra clearly, ensuring maximum damage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - enthusiastically doing the worst possible thing. Great dramatic irony.

jokes.text: Just like the noble buffalo. Nothing like a buffalo.
jokes.punchline: Leela immediately correcting that it's nothing like a buffalo
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor Farnsworth moment - making completely wrong analogies. Leela's correction is immediate and definitive.

jokes.text: Souls? Don't be ridiculous. It's their life forces that have been stolen. It's scientific.
jokes.punchline: Professor dismissing 'souls' as ridiculous while describing identical concept as 'life forces'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Professor character moment. Rejecting religious concept while embracing identical scientific-sounding one.

jokes.text: He has no soul. Life force!
jokes.punchline: Immediately correcting himself from 'soul' to 'life force'
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the Professor's obsession with maintaining scientific terminology.

jokes.text: If we poke a hole in Cobb's bag, will they spew out like a volcano built on an Indian graveyard?
jokes.setup: Asking how to restore the life forces
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific horror movie reference for soul restoration
jokes.explanation: Strange but vivid imagery combining supernatural elements. The specificity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Lo, the worm has turned.
jokes.punchline: Calculon's overly dramatic way of saying the tables have turned
jokes.explanation: Perfect Calculon over-dramatic speech. Archaic phrasing fits his theatrical character perfectly.

jokes.text: That's Romeo and Juliet, dummy, by Will.I.Am Shakespeare.
jokes.punchline: Attributing it to will.i.am instead of William Shakespeare
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining William Shakespeare with will.i.am. Good anachronistic humor for Futurama.

jokes.text: Food coloring? The most poisonous substance known to robots?
jokes.punchline: Food coloring being deadly to robots
jokes.explanation: Great world-building humor. Something harmless to humans being deadly to robots is perfectly logical absurdity.

jokes.text: I'll swap it out for harmless battery acid.
jokes.setup: Trying to fix the poison problem
jokes.punchline: Battery acid being 'harmless' to robots
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the food coloring joke. Reverses our expectations of what's harmful.

jokes.text: I'm a celebrity. I can kill anyone I want!
jokes.setup: Being told he can't kill himself
jokes.punchline: Claiming celebrity privilege extends to murder
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about celebrity privilege taken to logical extreme. Perfect Calculon ego and entitlement.

jokes.text: I always come in stinky.
jokes.punchline: Bender's response with sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Simple innuendo humor. Fits Bender's character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I always come in stinky.
jokes.punchline: Bender's response has unintended sexual connotation
jokes.explanation: Simple innuendo that fits Bender's crude personality. Efficient double entendre.

jokes.text: Whoof! I beg to differ.
jokes.punchline: Amy's disgusted reaction and polite disagreement
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to the innuendo. Amy's polite way of saying he actually does smell bad.

jokes.text: It's going totally Kanye!
jokes.punchline: Using 'Kanye' as a verb for ego inflation
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference using Kanye West's ego as a verb. Timely and quotable when it aired.

jokes.text: Ah! There's a humungous fungus among us.
jokes.punchline: Rhyming description of the creature
jokes.explanation: Fun rhyming description. 'Humungous fungus among us' has good rhythm and alliteration.

jokes.text: That's right, I said it. I said it!
jokes.setup: Calculon revealing gay relationship in death scene
jokes.punchline: Dramatically emphasizes his brave confession
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Calculon's need for dramatic emphasis even in death.

jokes.text: At least he died knowing I was great.
jokes.setup: Calculon actually dying in his performance
jokes.punchline: His final thought being about his own greatness, not the tragedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Calculon moment - even in death, his ego comes first. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: It just has to warm up for about 15 minutes.
jokes.setup: Urgent need to use anti-fungal ray
jokes.punchline: Device needs impractically long warm-up time during crisis
jokes.explanation: Good timing humor - high-tech weapon failing at crucial moment due to mundane limitation.

jokes.text: That ego must be at least four feet tall by now.
jokes.setup: Cobb basking in admiration after winning
jokes.punchline: Measuring ego literally by physical size
jokes.explanation: Good literalization of 'big ego' concept. Makes the abstract physical threat measurable.

jokes.text: Aw, did you miss me, pookie?
jokes.punchline: Talking to giant mushroom like a beloved pet
jokes.explanation: Creepy-cute moment treating the monstrous ego like a pet. Good contrast between appearance and treatment.

jokes.text: And everybody knows, once you delete a photo, it's gone forever.
jokes.setup: Needing the photo but having torn it up
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically stating obvious falsehood about digital photos
jokes.explanation: Good tech humor about digital photo permanence vs. film negatives. Sets up the solution.

jokes.text: Maybe a digital photo, but with film, I still have the negative.
jokes.setup: Seeming like the solution is impossible
jokes.punchline: Film photography's analog nature saves the day
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that makes Bender's pretentious film preference actually matter to the plot.

jokes.text: Eh. Contrast's a little off on this one.
jokes.setup: Racing to save friends from supernatural threat
jokes.punchline: Still perfectionist about photo quality during emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency. Even in crisis, Bender maintains his artistic pretensions.

jokes.text: The process.
jokes.punchline: Using his trademark pretentious acting term for alien parasitism
jokes.explanation: Great callback turning the actor's pretentious catchphrase into literal alien behavior description.

jokes.text: If we do, will you stop talking about the process?
jokes.setup: Cobb threatening them to look at him
jokes.punchline: More concerned about stopping his pretentious speech than dying
jokes.explanation: Great meta-humor about the audience's frustration with the character's repetitive catchphrase.

jokes.text: Oh, God, just kill us already!
jokes.setup: Cobb continuing to talk about 'the process'
jokes.punchline: Amy prefers death to more pretentious actor talk
jokes.explanation: Perfect audience surrogate reaction to pretentious actor dialogue. Relatable frustration.

jokes.text: start squirting out some soul-a-rrhea.
jokes.punchline: Combining 'soul' with 'diarrhea' for supernatural excretion
jokes.explanation: Crude but creative portmanteau. Gross humor that fits the supernatural situation.

jokes.text: The contrast is exquisite. And the composition... Sublime.
jokes.setup: Looking at photo that should destroy him
jokes.punchline: Instead of being destroyed, he critiques it like art
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - instead of destroying him, the photo feeds his ego more. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: My great photo only made his ego bigger.
jokes.punchline: Admitting his skill as photographer worsened the situation
jokes.explanation: Great irony - Bender's photographic skill becomes the problem. Perfect escalation of the threat.

jokes.text: You'll never be able to pay me back.
jokes.punchline: Immediately calculating the owed debt
jokes.explanation: Pure Bender - even heroic moments become about what he's owed. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: Say: 'Bender is great.' Bender is great!
jokes.setup: Taking group photo to commemorate the moment
jokes.punchline: Making everyone say his catchphrase instead of 'cheese'
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending that ties back to Bender's photography hobby and ego. Good character-consistent finale.

tmdb_overview: Bender joins the paparazzi and attempts to photograph a famous actor whose face has never been seen.
